lectures.summary: Bahasa Indonesia, or the language of Indonesia, is spoken by more than 250 million people in Southeast Asia, primarily Indonesia, Malaysia and Singapore.
lectures.description: Bahasa Indonesia, or the language of Indonesia, is spoken by more than 250 million people in Southeast Asia, primarily Indonesia, Malaysia and Singapore. Historically a trading language that linked people across maritime Southeast Asia, contemporary Indonesian is relatively easy to pick up for basic communication purposes, whether you’re planning a holiday in Bali or a business trip to Jakarta.
lectures.location: Sir Owen G Building, Auckland Business School, Room 349, Level 3, Auckland

Doctoral candidates from the Faculty of Business and Economics and the Faculty of Law are invited to compete in our joint Three Minute Thesis (3MT) Heat. lectures.description: Thailand, often referred to as “The Land of Smiles,” is known for its rich cultural heritage, striking temples, world-renowned cuisine, and welcoming communities. Learning Thai provides a meaningful way to experience this culture more deeply, whether for travel, personal interest, or future engagement with Thailand. Course Overview This introductory course is designed for complete beginners and those preparing to travel to Thailand.

lectures.title: Prompt Poets Society: April's Tools - AI Engineering Auckland
lectures.summary: The cogs of AI progress keep a turning! Join us as we discuss the latest and greatest with our April 2026 Prompt Poets Society.
lectures.description: The cogs of AI progress keep a turning! Join us as we discuss the latest and greatest with our April 2026 Prompt Poets Society. We will convene again at the 2degrees headquarters on Fanshawe St on the 28th of April at 6.00pm. Let's gather to discuss and swap notes on AI- assisted coding. We aspire to a collegial atmosphere with a mix of prepared talks and open discussion. Agenda: 6.00pm - Enjoy pizza from Totos and beverages of all kinds (thanks to GD1 and New and Improved) 6.30pm - Intro, house keeping and a few words from our sponsors 6.40pm - Lauren Peate (Multitudes) - How teams get more from AI: Research-backed insights 7.20pm - Short break 7.40pm - Nicolas Maquet (SigmaBench) - By agents, for agents - lessons from building an agent-native codebase 8.30pm - Wrap up Location: 2degrees, 136 Fanshawe Street, Auckland Central, Auckland 1010 Totos Pizza and Drinks (Beer, wine and non-alcoholic drinks) provided Thanks to [GD1](https://www.gd1.vc) and [New and Improved](https://newandimproved.ventures) for providing Pizza and Drinks Shout out to [2degrees](https://www.2degrees.nz) for hosting ❤️
lectures.location: 2degrees Head Office, 136 Fanshawe Street, Auckland

name: NZ Institute of International Affairs
description: The New Zealand Institute of International Affairs hosts public lectures and forums on foreign policy, geopolitics, and international relations across New Zealand.

lectures.title: NZIIA Akl Branch AGM 2026 + presentation on NZ’s Trade Policy During an Era of Geoeconomics and Global Conflict
lectures.summary: We would like to invite you to attend the NZIIA Auckland Branch’s Annual General Meeting for 2026, which will be held on Tuesday 28th April 2026.
lectures.description: We would like to invite you to attend the NZIIA Auckland Branch’s Annual General Meeting for 2026, which will be held on Tuesday 28th April 2026. During this AGM we will be sharing about how the past year has been, thank the current committee, update everyone on what the upcoming year will look like with exciting events and also election of a new committee, and meet the new Executive Director of NZIIA! If you are interested in joining the new Auckland Branch committee, please send your nominations and self-nominations to the current Chairperson of NZIIA’s Auckland Branch Francesca Long ( francescalong111@gmail.com ) by the 26th April . After the AGM finishes at around 6:30pm, we would love you to also meet the new Executive Director of NZIIA, Catherine Grant Makokera! With a wealth of experience, knowledge and expertise in International Trade, Catherine will be sharing on the topic of New Zealand’s Trade Policy During an Era of Geoeconomics and Global Conflict. With all the conflicts taking place currently, and disruptions to supply chains felt globally at all levels, attendees are in for a treat with Catherine’s timely presentation, with the opportunity to also ask questions at the end. We look forward to seeing you there! Please sign up, as there will be limited seats. And please notify the Chair if you are unable to make it after registering so that we can reopen a spot. Bio: Catherine is an experienced international relations and economic diplomacy expert having worked in the public, private and academic sectors. She is currently the Executive Director of the New Zealand Institute of International Affairs. She has extensive experience as an international development consultant, heading teams of experts supporting a wide range of projects in the areas of trade and economics in Southern and Eastern Africa, the Pacific and New Zealand. Catherine was a diplomat for New Zealand for 10 years, posted in New York, Geneva and South Africa. She participated in United Nations, International Labour Organisation and World Trade Organisation negotiations. She is a Research Associate of the Public Policy Institute at Auckland University. Important branch documents for your viewing: AGM Agenda Minutes of the 2025 Branch AGM Branch Chair’s Report for 2025 and summary of 2025’s Branch events
lectures.location: Kinder House, 2 Ayr Street, Parnell, Auckland

lectures.title: The Fall of Saigon - 50 years on
lectures.summary: Mr Wilson is a former senior New Zealand diplomat, with a strong career background in Asia. The topic of his talk will be The Fall of Saigon - 50 years On.
lectures.description: Mr Wilson is a former senior New Zealand diplomat, with a strong career background in Asia. The topic of his talk will be The Fall of Saigon - 50 years On. Frank Wilson witnessed the fall of Saigon in April 1975. He will talk about his own experiences, that of some other New Zealanders there at the time, and what the fall of Saigon meant for New Zealand, and its wider regional and international implications. As usual, our meeting will be held at the Havelock North Club (8 Campbell Street), starting at 2pm, running through until about 3.30pm (including afternoon tea). The standard door charge of $8 will apply, to cover venue and afternoon tea costs.
lectures.location: Havelock North Club, 4 Campbell Street, Havelock North

lectures.title: NZIIA AGM and Annual Lecture 2026
lectures.summary: NZIIA members are warmly invited to attend the national Annual General Meeting of the New Zealand Institute of International Affairs, followed by our annual lecture.
lectures.description: NZIIA members are warmly invited to attend the national Annual General Meeting of the New Zealand Institute of International Affairs, followed by our annual lecture. TIMING: 4-5pm, AGM including an address by NZIIA Chair Dr James Kember and Executive Director - at at RHMZ03, Mezzanine Level, Rutherford House, 33 Bunny Street Wellington 6-7pm, Annual lecture, with Hon Todd McClay, Minister for Trade and Investment - at the Grand Hall, Parliament Buildings, Molesworth Street. 7pm - 8pm, drinks reception - at the Grand Hall, Parliament Buildings, Molesworth Street. Free to attend, although contributions towards event costs and ongoing running of NZIIA are welcome. If you are attending both the AGM and Annual Lecture, please register for both options. There were 11 nominations for three NZIIA Board places up for election this year. Members will be notified of the election results at the AGM. The AGM Agenda is available here . The minutes of the last AGM held in 2025 are available here . Corporate and institutional members should specify in writing who their appointed delegate is for the AGM. This information, notice of attendance at the AGM and forms for proxy voting at the AGM can be received by the Executive Director up to 1 hour before the start of the event. Please send apologies to nziia@vuw.ac.nz.
lectures.location: Room 3, Mezzanine Level (RHMZ03), Rutherford House, 33 Bunny Street, Wellington, 6011 and Parliament Buildings (and online via Zoom)

speakers.name: Hon Todd McClay, Minister for Trade and Investment

lectures.description: Public Record workshop at our gallery space     Kintsugi (Modernised - Mending method / Art form) Kintsugi (also known as Kintsukuroi) which loosely translates to 'Golden Joinery' is the age-old Japanese art form of repairing broken ceramics in a way that is often considered more beautiful than when it was when whole.

lectures.title: Printopia Festival
lectures.summary: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific.
lectures.description: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific. Now a recognised regional arts event, the Festival presents its signature programme including the country’s largest print fair, live printing, artist talks and hands-on workshops. New for 2026 are curated exhibitions, guided gallery tours and experimental performances that expand how print is experienced. Taking place across four central Auckland venues, Printopia invites audiences to connect, explore and be inspired.
lectures.location: Studio One Toi Tū, 1 Summer Street, Grey Lynn, Auckland

lectures.title: Women & Non-binary Artist Hui: Cyanotype Workshop
lectures.summary: In this cyanotype workshop you’ll discover how science and creativity can coincide.
lectures.description: In this cyanotype workshop you’ll discover how science and creativity can coincide. Experiment with exposure, shadow, and UV light to create your own unique print using natural materials gathered from your surroundings. Lulu will guide you through two hours of playful exploration, learning, and good chats, perfect for anyone keen to try something new and make art in a relaxed, supportive environment.
lectures.location: Studio One Toi Tū, 1 Summer Street, Grey Lynn, Auckland

lectures.title: Tā: Siapo as a Living Practice
lectures.summary: This two-day workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making as a living practice.
lectures.description: This two-day workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making as a living practice. Through talanoa, we will explore the history of siapo in Sāmoa, its cultural significance and the ways it continues to be reimagined today.
lectures.location: Studio One Toi Tū, 1 Summer Street, Grey Lynn, Auckland

lectures.title: Mamalu – A Lineage Embodied
lectures.summary: This workshop centres the malu, the Samoan female tattoo, as a living archive of cultural knowledge and ancestral presence.
lectures.description: This workshop centres the malu, the Samoan female tattoo, as a living archive of cultural knowledge and ancestral presence. Through discussion, drawing, writing, and embodied practice, participants will explore how Indigenous knowledge is encoded in the body, and how these markings intersect with gender, identity, and sovereignty. Grounded Rosanna's own malu and artistic practice, the workshop invites critical reflection on issues of cultural appropriation, authorship, and the responsibilities of working with Indigenous visual systems.
lectures.location: Studio One Toi Tū, 1 Summer Street, Grey Lynn, Auckland

lectures.title: Siva Mo Lou Soifua Manuia
lectures.summary: This workshop offers a nurturing journey into Siva Samoa, where movement supports wellbeing.
lectures.description: This workshop offers a nurturing journey into Siva Samoa, where movement supports wellbeing. Participants explore foundational Siva Samoa steps and their cultural meanings — gestures that honour connections to land, ocean, sky, ancestry, and community. Through soft hands, grounded feet, and mindful breath, they experience how movement mirrors the natural world. Rooted in traditional practice, the session invites people to slow down, reconnect, and honour the body. Open to all levels. Let them Siva for health, language, and collective wellbeing.
lectures.location: Studio One Toi Tū, 1 Summer Street, Grey Lynn, Auckland

speakers.name: Professor Stefano Pampanin
speakers.bio: Chair in Structural Engineering, University of Canterbury

lectures.title: New Zealand's Space Future: From Rocket Lab to National Strategy
lectures.summary: An exploration of New Zealand's rapidly growing space sector, the science enabled by domestic launch capability, and the policy frameworks governing our use of space.
lectures.description: From seminars and lectures to arts and sporting events there are lots of interesting events happening at UC all the time! See what's on or search for an event.
lectures.location: E9 Lecture Theatre, Science Road, University of Canterbury, Christchurch

lectures.title: Canterbury Water: Ecology, Irrigation, and the Future of Our Freshwater
lectures.summary: Researchers and community voices discuss the competing demands on Canterbury's freshwater resources and what a sustainable future looks like for the region.
lectures.description: From seminars and lectures to arts and sporting events there are lots of interesting events happening at UC all the time! See what's on or search for an event.
lectures.location: von Haast Building, University of Canterbury, Christchurch

name: University of Otago
description: New Zealand's first university, founded in 1869. Otago hosts distinguished lectures, public seminars, and community events across its Dunedin, Christchurch, and Wellington campuses.

lectures.title: Distinguished Lecture: The Brain in Pain — New Frontiers in Chronic Pain Research
lectures.summary: The annual Otago Distinguished Lecture in Medicine explores breakthrough research into the neuroscience of chronic pain and emerging treatments.

lectures.title: Secrets of the Southern Ocean: Otago's Deep-Sea Research Programme
lectures.summary: Marine scientists share discoveries from recent voyages to the Southern Ocean, including new species, ecosystem dynamics, and implications for fisheries management.

lectures.title: Equity in Health: Progress and Challenges for Māori Wellbeing
lectures.summary: A wānanga-style public discussion on the persistent health inequities facing Māori communities and what genuine system change would require.

speakers.name: Professor Papaarangi Reid
speakers.bio: Dean of Te Ara Hauora, Faculty of Medical and Health Sciences

name: University of Waikato
description: The University of Waikato hosts free public lectures in Hamilton and Tauranga, featuring professorial lectures, research talks, and community events across its campuses.

lectures.title: Hamilton Public Lecture by Professor Nicola Daly
lectures.summary: We often underestimate picturebooks, but the truth is they reflect what we want to pass on to our tamariki-mokopuna; they are incredibly powerful.
lectures.description: We often underestimate picturebooks, but the truth is they reflect what we want to pass on to our tamariki-mokopuna; they are incredibly powerful.
lectures.location: Gallagher Academy of Performing Arts, Hamilton

lectures.title: Hamilton Public Lecture by Professor Judy Bowen
lectures.summary: Technology is embedded into every part of our lives, but what can we do to make certain that it is reliable, usable and safe?
lectures.description: Technology is embedded into every part of our lives, but what can we do to make certain that it is reliable, usable and safe?
lectures.location: Gallagher Academy of Performing Arts, Hamilton

lectures.title: Regulatory Law through the Lens of Law-in-Context' by Dr Simon Robb
lectures.summary: Dr Simon Robb has academic foundations in sociology and law and draws on his regulatory career to explore law-in-context insights that deepen understanding of governance and regulatory practice.
lectures.description: The University of Waikato is a leading New Zealand university with campuses in Hamilton and Tauranga. We offer teaching and research across diverse disciplines, and support a vibrant community of students, academics, researchers, and global partners.
